message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-ec18d (last 11 fnx wrapper refactor)

Fixed in commit b58a6db. NX_DELEGATED dropped 15 → 4 (just nx
re-exports remain).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -53,7 +53,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-d967f","title":"parity: approximation treewidth/steiner/dominating/max-cut/ramsey/densest wrappers missing (10 functions)","description":"After the TSP family (br-8vobd), 10 more approximation algorithms remain unexposed: treewidth_min_degree(G), treewidth_min_fill_in(G), steiner_tree(G, terminal_nodes, weight, method), min_edge_dominating_set(G), min_maximal_matching(G), min_weighted_dominating_set(G, weight), one_exchange(G, initial_cut, seed, weight), randomized_partitioning(G, seed, p, weight), ramsey_R2(G), densest_subgraph(G, iterations, method). All are pure heuristics with no Rust counterpart — add wrappers via the existing _call_networkx_approximation_for_parity helper, export at top-level, add to __all__ under the approximation section.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T19:34:18.988742298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T19:57:48.971397375Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T19:57:48.969471288Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 66ec413: 10 approximation wrappers (treewidth_min_degree/min_fill_in, steiner_tree, min_edge_dominating_set, min_maximal_matching, min_weighted_dominating_set, one_exchange, randomized_partitioning, ramsey_R2, densest_subgraph). All verified against nx on K5/C6/P6/K6/C7/BA20 graphs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-dc14n","title":"Graph.edges(nbunch) returns edges in wrong order vs nx","description":"fnx.Graph.edges(nbunch) (and .edges(node)) returns edges in a different iteration order than nx. The all-edges (no nbunch) case matches; only the filtered case drifts.

Repro:
  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','e'),('a','c')]  
  Both libs: adj['a'] = ['b','c']
  
  fnx.edges(['a','b']) -> [('b','c'), ('a','c'), ('a','b')]
  nx .edges(['a','b']) -> [('a','b'), ('a','c'), ('b','c')]
  
  fnx.edges('a') -> [('a','c'), ('a','b')]
  nx .edges('a') -> [('a','b'), ('a','c')]   (matches adj['a'] order)
  
  fnx.edges(['a','b','c']) -> [('c','d'), ('c','b'), ('c','a'), ('a','b')]
  nx .edges(['a','b','c']) -> [('a','b'), ('a','c'), ('b','c'), ('c','d')]

nx's contract: iterate nbunch_list in user-given order, for each node yield edges (u, v) in adj[u] order, skipping edges where v is already-seen. fnx's Rust path appears to iterate G.nodes() (full graph) and filter by nbunch — completely different.

Drop-in code that iterated G.edges(nbunch) expecting nx's adj-ordered output broke. DiGraph cases (edges, in_edges, out_edges) match — only undirected edges() with nbunch drifts.

Fix: override EdgeDataView._materialize to iterate nbunch in user order with adj-iteration, mirroring nx's exact algorithm.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:42:44.701569209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:49:57.853658449Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T17:49:57.851586465Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-dgicx","title":"florentine_families_graph node + edge + adj order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.florentine_families_graph builds the graph with edges in alphabetical/canonical order, but nx uses a specific narrative-order list of add_edge calls (Castellani's edges before Medici's middle edges, etc.). This produces different node-insertion order, edge iteration order, and per-node adj order.

Repro:
  fnx nodes: ['Acciaiuoli','Medici','Albizzi','Ginori','Guadagni','Barbadori','Castellani','Bischeri',...]
  nx  nodes: ['Acciaiuoli','Medici','Castellani','Peruzzi','Strozzi','Barbadori','Ridolfi','Tornabuoni',...]

  fnx adj['Medici']: ['Acciaiuoli','Albizzi','Barbadori','Ridolfi','Salviati','Tornabuoni']
  nx  adj['Medici']: ['Acciaiuoli','Barbadori','Ridolfi','Tornabuoni','Albizzi','Salviati']

Drop-in code that iterated this classic dataset's nodes/edges in nx order broke. Fix: replicate nx's exact add_edge sequence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:03:25.731453322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:05:45.516706801Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:05:45.514515137Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-ec18d","title":"Refactor 11 remaining fnx wrappers out of NX_DELEGATED","description":"11 public wrappers still classify as NX_DELEGATED:
- 4 RCM ordering: cuthill_mckee_ordering, reverse_cuthill_mckee_ordering, connected_cuthill_mckee_ordering, pseudo_peripheral_node
- 2 branchings: branching_weight, minimal_branching
- 1 flow: flow_matrix_row
- 1 matching: matching_dict_to_set
- 3 misc: is_valid_tree_degree_sequence, geometric_soft_configuration_graph, random_uniform_k_out_graph

Each currently imports nx + calls nx.* directly. Refactor each to use _xxx_via_nx private helper. Drops NX_DELEGATED count by 11 (15 → 4). The remaining 4 (draw_networkx_*) are nx re-exports — different category, out of scope.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T21:36:59.423898032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T21:37:57.573007231Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-ec18d","title":"Refactor 11 remaining fnx wrappers out of NX_DELEGATED","description":"11 public wrappers still classify as NX_DELEGATED:
- 4 RCM ordering: cuthill_mckee_ordering, reverse_cuthill_mckee_ordering, connected_cuthill_mckee_ordering, pseudo_peripheral_node
- 2 branchings: branching_weight, minimal_branching
- 1 flow: flow_matrix_row
- 1 matching: matching_dict_to_set
- 3 misc: is_valid_tree_degree_sequence, geometric_soft_configuration_graph, random_uniform_k_out_graph

Each currently imports nx + calls nx.* directly. Refactor each to use _xxx_via_nx private helper. Drops NX_DELEGATED count by 11 (15 → 4). The remaining 4 (draw_networkx_*) are nx re-exports — different category, out of scope.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T21:36:59.423898032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T21:40:24.923599863Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T21:40:24.921543268Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-f47su","title":"parity: cut_size always returns float, nx returns int when result is integer","description":"fnx.cut_size always returns a float (1.0, 2.0) regardless of input weight type. nx.cut_size preserves int when result is integer (returns 1, 2). For unweighted graphs, nx returns int (just edge count). Drop-in code that asserts isinstance(cut_size(G, S), int) breaks. Fix: detect integer result (or all-int weights) and coerce return value back to int.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:14:20.019169305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:16:27.958106136Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:16:27.956009365Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 7a9c308. cut_size preserves int return type when input weights are int (or unweighted) matching nx exactly. 7 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-f64cy","title":"parity: pagerank silently falls back to unweighted when weight attr is missing","description":"fnx.pagerank(G, weight='nope') on a graph whose edges lack a 'nope' attribute returns the unweighted result instead of raising. NetworkX defaults the missing attribute to 1 (treated as edge weight 1) — semantically same as unweighted but only when ALL edges lack the key. fnx behavior is similar but should be verified against nx for partial-attribute graphs (some edges weighted, others missing). Add parity test that builds a graph where half the edges have weight, half don't, and asserts fnx and nx agree to 1e-9.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:26:41.408497679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:11:17.258068772Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T09:11:17.256114832Z","close_reason":"Verified parity. fnx.pagerank already matches nx exactly on per-edge default-1 fallback for missing weight attributes — no code fix needed. Landed tests/python/test_pagerank_weight_parity.py (commit 400eefe) with 6 cases pinning the contract: all-weighted, weight='nope' (all missing), partial weights (half missing), weight=None, DiGraph partial, plus a sanity check that asymmetric weights produce a non-trivial pagerank delta vs unweighted (proving the implementation isn't in a hidden global fallback path).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-f6epo","title":"parity: second_order_centrality dict iterates in wrong node order","description":"fnx.second_order_centrality returns a dict in arbitrary internal order (e.g. for path_graph(5): [2, 4, 0, 1, 3]) instead of nx's node-insertion order [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]. Same iteration-order drift as br-r37-c1-pm78h (communicability_betweenness_centrality). Drop-in code that iterates 'for n, score in centrality.items(): ...' breaks. Values are unchanged. Fix: reorder result dict in iteration order of G.nodes().","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:17:46.842301576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:19:51.423520818Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:19:51.421530578Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 58665d3. second_order_centrality now iterates in node-insertion order matching nx. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
